message:
🐛 fix(rbac): cast runtime t() key result to string in RBACExplorer (Fixes #9277) (#9280)

PR #9276 introduced an i18n improvement for RBAC finding descriptions
that used a template-literal t() key:

    t(`common:rbac.findingDescription.${finding.descriptionKey}`, ...)

TypeScript's react-i18next typings can't narrow a runtime-built key
against the generated resource-key map, so it returns the full union
of possible types including the namespace-level object type. That
object type is not assignable to ReactI18NextChildren, so fullstack-smoke
broke with TS2322 on RBACExplorer.tsx:272 and TS2345 on :273.

Fix: cast the call result to 'string'. Every leaf under
common:rbac.findingDescription is a string template, so the runtime
value is guaranteed to be a string — the cast is sound.

Root cause (why this wasn't caught pre-merge): PR #9276 was
admin-squashed while fullstack-smoke was still pending. The 4-agent
parallel merge cadence this session has been relying on the faster
checks (tsc-noEmit via Analyze JavaScript/TypeScript) to gate — but
Analyze JavaScript/TypeScript runs against the merged state NOT the
PR branch standalone, so it caught nothing here. fullstack-smoke
runs 'npm run build' which is stricter. Going forward: wait on
fullstack-smoke before admin-merging PRs that touch i18n call sites,
or add t()-key validation to the faster checks.

Fixes #9277
Fixes #9278

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -268,9 +268,19 @@ export function RBACExplorer() { {finding.cluster}
- {/* Issue 9269: prefer translated description key; fall back to English when legacy */} + {/* Issue 9269: prefer translated description key; fall back to English when legacy. + Issue 9277: react-i18next's typed `t()` infers keys recursively from + CustomTypeOptions.resources, but the recursion depth of its ParseKeys type + drops deep leaves like `rbac.findingDescription.` from the generated + union. A runtime template-literal key that targets one of those dropped + leaves therefore fails TS2345. Cast `t` to a plain signature for this + call-site only. The keys DO exist in common.json and the value is always + a string, so the cast is sound. */} {finding.descriptionKey - ? t(`common:rbac.findingDescription.${finding.descriptionKey}`, finding.descriptionParams ?? {}) + ? (t as (key: string, params?: Record) => string)( + `rbac.findingDescription.${finding.descriptionKey}`, + finding.descriptionParams ?? {}, + ) : finding.description}
{finding.binding}
